blueprint_markdown:
## Executive Summary

Summit Medical Academy's organic footprint is anchored almost entirely in Boise/Idaho market terms, a geography that belongs to the sister brand Idaho Medical Academy, not to SMA's expansion mandate. The site ranks for 70 keywords at a mean position of 46, with the strongest signals concentrated on `/aemt-course-online/` (22 keywords, 1,940 monthly volume) and `/about-us/` (12 keywords, 830 monthly volume). The about page is absorbing high-intent commercial queries — `emt training boise` (pos 8), `boise emt training` (pos 9), `idaho emt courses` (pos 74), `emt training idaho` (pos 16) — that it cannot convert. The single largest recoverable gap is the absence of a dedicated online EMT-B course page: `online emt course` (9,900/mo, $6.31 CPC) and `emt training online` (9,900/mo, $6.31 CPC) are both unranked, while the closest landing candidate, `/aemt-course-online/`, ranks at position 74 for `emt course online` (2,400/mo) — a structural mismatch between URL and user intent. SMA's six designated expansion states — Washington, Oregon, California, Utah, Arizona, and Montana — contribute zero tracked rankings and have zero corresponding pages.

The architecture organizes around four content silos: Online EMT Course (national hub + seven state cluster pages), AEMT Course (national hub + state clusters), EMT Continuing Education and Recertification (national pillar + state recertification clusters), and NREMT Test Prep and Career Support (single-pillar with supporting content). A fifth silo consolidates State and Regional EMT Training under one geo-agnostic pillar, absorbing the commercial intent currently misrouted through `/about-us/` and scattered across event URLs. A sixth silo addresses Enrollment Support and Trust, housing FAQ, cost, and comparison content that supports decision-stage queries across all service categories. The highest single-page priority is the `/online-emt-course/` national hub, which must be built before state variants go live so internal link authority can flow down the hub-and-spoke structure. The committed architecture's 28 pages are fully absorbed across these six silos; seven previously deprecated entries have been evaluated and retained with corrected roles, and two gap items identified in the Content Gap Intelligence — AEMT geo pages for Oregon and Montana, and the national CE pillar — are addressed by existing committed pages that the gap analysis did not recognize.

The site runs on WordPress with a confirmed structural crawlability problem: all fifteen crawled pages report zero unique inlinks, indicating JavaScript-rendered navigation that prevents Googlebot from discovering internal link structure via static HTML. Two blocking issues must be resolved before any new page is published: (1) the XML sitemap must be generated via Yoast SEO or Rank Math and submitted to Google Search Console, or new pages will not be reliably crawled; (2) brand entity consolidation must be completed — nine of fifteen pages carry "Idaho Medical Academy" in title tags on the `summitmedicalacademy.com` domain, creating an entity resolution failure that suppresses authority for every new page built on top of it. These are not Phase 2 tasks. Additionally, the homepage hero WebP image at 4.8MB is a probable LCP failure at the template level; every state landing page will inherit this liability if the image compression issue is not resolved before the expansion build begins. Phase 2 expansion (city-level pages below state hubs, AEMT cost pages per state, Idaho recertification page) is noted in the appropriate silos and deferred until Phase 1 state infrastructure is indexed and showing ranking signal.

## Platform Constraints

**CMS:** WordPress (confirmed — `wp-content/uploads/` image paths, `?page_id=` 404 patterns, `.webp` media library filenames).

**URL slug format:** WordPress supports nested paths via custom post types or page hierarchy (e.g., `online-emt-course/california` as a child page under `online-emt-course`). The committed architecture uses nested slugs for all state variants under `online-emt-course/`. These must be created as child pages in WordPress with the parent page set to `online-emt-course`. Do not create these as flat top-level pages or the URL structure will break the hub-and-spoke architecture.

**Navigation crawlability (BLOCKING):** All crawled pages show zero unique inlinks, indicating the primary navigation is JavaScript-rendered and invisible to Googlebot. Before any new pages are published, verify via `view-source:` that `<a href>` tags are present in static HTML for all navigation links. If the theme or page builder (likely Elementor, Divi, or WPBakery based on DOM size and response times) is injecting navigation via JavaScript, either switch to a static menu or add a static HTML fallback nav. This is a prerequisite — not a post-launch fix.

**Sitemap (BLOCKING):** No XML sitemap exists. Install Yoast SEO or Rank Math immediately. Configure the sitemap to include all service pages and exclude event archive pages, author pages, and out-of-scope category pages. Submit to Google Search Console before any new page is published.

**Structured data:** WordPress does not generate `Course`, `EducationalOrganization`, `FAQPage`, or `BreadcrumbList` schema natively. All structured data must be implemented via Rank Math (has built-in Course schema support), Yoast (requires custom JSON-LD blocks), or direct JSON-LD injection in the theme's `<head>`. Given the volume of schema types required across 28+ pages, Rank Math's schema builder is the recommended implementation path.

**Broken `?page_id=` URLs:** Three 404 URLs with `?page_id=` parameters appear in the live link graph. Audit in WordPress admin → Pages → All Pages and remove or redirect any draft/deleted pages that are still linked from published content.

**`/upcoming-emt-classes/` 404:** This URL returns a 404 and is linked internally. Apply a 301 redirect to `/upcoming-advanced-emt-classes/` immediately to preserve any inbound link equity and eliminate the crawl budget drain.

**Image compression:** The 4.8MB homepage hero WebP is a template-level LCP failure. Install Imagify, ShortPixel, or EWWW Image Optimizer and run a batch compression pass before building state pages that inherit the same template.

---

### Silo 1: Online EMT Course — National Hub and State Clusters

This silo targets the highest-volume commercial opportunity in the dataset: national geo-agnostic online EMT-B queries (`online emt course`, `emt course online`, `emt training online` — combined ~29,700/mo) plus state-modified variants across all seven SMA service markets. The national hub page is the pillar; all state pages are cluster pages that receive internal link authority from the hub. The current `/aemt-course-online/` page is cannibalizing this traffic — that conflict is addressed in Cannibalization Warnings. The committed architecture's `emt-b-online-course`, `emt-course-cost`, `how-online-emt-training-works`, and `online-emt-course/cost` pages are absorbed as support pages within this silo. Note: `online-emt-course/cost` (committed, in_progress) is the primary cost support page; `emt-course-cost` (committed, deprecated) is a duplicate target and is redirected rather than built separately — see Deprecation Candidates.

| Retention (recertification, renewal) | Not applicable | — | Retention is handled in Silo 3 (CE/Recertification) |

### Silo 2: AEMT Course — Advanced EMT Certification

This silo owns all AEMT-specific content. The existing `/aemt-course-online/` page has genuine authority — it holds position 3 for `online advanced emt course` (80/mo) — but is currently being misused as a catch-all for EMT-B online queries. Once the Silo 1 hub absorbs EMT-B traffic, this page can be restructured to serve only AEMT intent. State cluster pages for all six expansion states plus Idaho are added as child pages. The committed `aemt-certification-requirements` page is included as a support page. An `aemt-vs-emt` comparison page addresses the documented Content Gap Intelligence format gap and the Consideration buyer stage. A `what-is-aemt` support page addresses the gap flagged in Content Gap Intelligence for AEMT scope-of-practice informational content and improves AI citation eligibility for `online advanced emt course` queries (current position 3, zero AI citations).

### Silo 3: EMT Continuing Education and Recertification

This silo captures the recurring-demand segment of working EMTs needing CE credits and NREMT recertification. The committed architecture includes `emt-continuing-education-online` and `emt-recertification-requirements` plus the deprecated `emt-training-[state]` pages. This silo reframes those state training pages: each state has both an EMT training/course cluster page (under Silo 5 for initial certification) and a recertification cluster page here for the renewal audience. The national CE pillar is the highest-priority build in this silo — `emt continuing education online` (590/mo, $6.77 CPC) plus `emt continuing education` (2,900/mo, $2.19 CPC) represent 3,490 combined monthly searches with zero current rankings and zero corresponding pages. California CE queries carry the highest CPC in the entire CE cluster ($9.93 CPC) and are addressed by the California recertification cluster page. The committed `emt-recertification-requirements` support page provides the national-level requirements overview that feeds all state variants.

### Silo 4: NREMT Test Prep and Career Support

This silo expands the existing `/nremt-test-prep/` page (currently ranking for only 2 keywords despite `nremt test prep` carrying 14,800/mo at $2.54 CPC) and adds career-outcome support content that captures top-of-funnel informational queries and improves AI citation eligibility. The committed `emt-career-outcomes`, `why-become-an-emt`, and `nremt-exam-locations` support pages are included here. The `nremt-exam-guide` page addresses the documented NREMT study guide format gap — `nremt study guide` and `how to pass nremt` are identified by Jim as competitor-dominated informational terms that SMA currently does not address. The `/why-you-should-get-emt-certified/` existing page is folded into this silo as the `why-become-an-emt` cluster; the existing URL should be preserved and optimized rather than replaced, with a 301 from `/why-you-should-get-emt-certified/` only if the slug is changed.

| URL Slug | Status | Silo | Role | Primary Keyword | Volume | Action |
|----------|--------|------|------|-----------------|--------|--------|
| nremt-test-prep | exists | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | pillar | nremt test prep | 14800 | optimize |
| nremt-exam-guide | new | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | cluster | how to pass nremt | est. | create |
| nremt-exam-locations | new | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | support | where to take the nremt test | 60 | create |
| emt-career-outcomes | new | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | cluster | what can you do with an emt certification | 120 | create |
| why-become-an-emt | exists | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | support | should i be an emt | 60 | optimize |
| become-an-emt | new | NREMT Test Prep and Career Support | cluster | how to become an emt in idaho | 10 | create |

**Note on `become-an-emt`:** This page addresses the Content Gap Intelligence Citation Gap 4 — the absence of certification pathway content. It should be structured with `HowTo` schema covering the NREMT-to-state-license process for all SMA service states (Idaho, WA, OR, CA, UT, AZ, MT). The primary keyword `how to become an emt in idaho` carries only 10/mo in the current dataset; use `how to become an emt` (est.) as the national frame and include state-specific H2 sections. This is an AI citation priority, not a volume play.

### Silo 5: State and Regional EMT Training

This silo provides geo-targeted landing pages for initial EMT course seekers searching state-modified training queries (`emt training california`, `emt course arizona`, etc.). These pages are distinct from the Online EMT Course state pages in Silo 1: Silo 1 pages target online-delivery-specific queries; Silo 5 pages target the broader `emt training [state]` and `emt course [state]` queries that may not specify delivery mode. The pillar is a geography-agnostic `/emt-training/` hub (retitled from the committed `emt-training-idaho` which previously served as a misassigned Idaho-only page). The committed `emt-course-boise` and `emt-course-in-person` pages are absorbed here as the Boise/Idaho local cluster. The committed `emt-training-[state]` deprecated pages are retained as cluster pages within this silo. The committed `how-to-become-an-emt-in-idaho` page is consolidated into the `become-an-emt` pathway page in Silo 4 — see Deprecation Candidates. The misrouted `/about-us/` commercial queries (`emt training boise`, `idaho emt courses`) are addressed by `emt-course-boise` and `emt-training-idaho` cluster pages here.

**Note on `emt-training-idaho` as pillar:** This page serves as both the Idaho state cluster and the entity-consolidation anchor for the IMA/SMA relationship. It should explicitly address SMA's relationship to Idaho Medical Academy, explain that SMA serves students outside Idaho while IMA serves Idaho-resident students, and include the `sameAs` + `parentOrganization` structured data linking both entities. This page absorbs the commercial Idaho queries currently misdirecting through `/about-us/`.

### Silo 6: Enrollment Support and Trust

This silo consolidates all conversion-support, FAQ, financial, and employer-facing content. The `/q-a/` page is restructured from its current out-of-scope CPR/AHA framing to serve EMT-specific FAQ content with FAQPage schema. The `/payment-plan-options/` page is retained and optimized with explicit cost benchmarks, scholarship information (NAEMT scholarship — currently ranking at position 23 for `naemt scholarships`), and schema. The `/employers/` page is retained as a B2B trust signal. The `/about-us/` page is stripped of commercial keyword signals and restructured as a pure entity-clarity and trust page with `EducationalOrganization` schema, `sameAs` links, and the SMA/IMA relationship statement. The `/instructors/` page is rebranded from IMA to SMA identity. The `emt-certification-meaning` support page addresses the committed `aemt-certification-requirements` informational angle for EMT-B (distinct from AEMT certification requirements in Silo 2).

| URL Slug | Status | Silo | Role | Primary Keyword | Volume | Action |
|----------|--------|------|------|-----------------|--------|--------|
| about-us | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | pillar | summit medical academy | 60 | optimize |
| q-a | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | cluster | emt course faq | est. | optimize |
| payment-plan-options | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | cluster | naemt scholarships | 60 | optimize |
| employers | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | support | boise emt jobs | 60 | optimize |
| about-us/instructors | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | support | emt instructors summit medical academy | est. | optimize |
| about-us/testimonials | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | support | idaho medical academy reviews | 50 | optimize |
| contact | exists | Enrollment Support and Trust | support | contact summit medical academy | est. | optimize |

**Note on `about-us` restructure:** This page must be stripped of all commercial keyword signals (`emt training boise`, `idaho emt courses`, `emt certification idaho`) that the crawl shows it currently absorbing. After restructure, it should carry `EducationalOrganization` JSON-LD, `sameAs` links to the Google Business Profile and IMA's domain, the SMA brand statement, accreditation details, and instructor credentials summary. It should not target any query a prospective student would use to find a course.

**Note on `q-a` restructure:** The current title tag targets "FAQ for CPR AHA" — an out-of-scope topic. Rewrite title to target EMT course FAQ questions. Restructure all Q&A content to cover `how long does emt course take`, `can i take emt online`, `what is aemt`, `emt course payment plan`, and state eligibility questions. Implement FAQPage schema on all question/answer pairs immediately — this is the highest-confidence schema gap in the crawl and the fastest path to Google AI Overview citation.

## Cannibalization Warnings

**1. `/aemt-course-online/` cannibalizes all online EMT-B queries**
Competing pages: `/aemt-course-online/` vs. the new `online-emt-course` pillar (Silo 1).
Keywords in conflict: `emt course online` (pos 74, 2,400/mo), `emt-b online course` (pos 48, 140/mo), `online emt course idaho` (pos 51, 30/mo), `emt online courses with certificate` (pos 73, 60/mo), `nremt course online` (pos 53, 60/mo).
Resolution: `online-emt-course` (Silo 1 pillar) owns all EMT-B online queries. After `online-emt-course` is published and indexed, `/aemt-course-online/` must be updated to remove all EMT-B framing from H1, meta description, body copy, and internal anchor text. The page should explicitly state it is an Advanced EMT (AEMT) course for already-certified EMT-Bs. Add a canonical cross-link: "Looking for the basic EMT course? See our Online EMT Course." No redirect — the AEMT page retains its own rankings for AEMT-specific queries.

**2. `/about-us/` misrouted for commercial training queries**
Competing pages: `/about-us/` vs. new `emt-course-boise` (Silo 5) and `emt-training-idaho` (Silo 5).
Keywords in conflict: `emt training boise` (pos 8), `boise emt training` (pos 9), `emt training idaho` (pos 16), `idaho emt courses` (pos 74), `idaho emt training` (pos 77), `emt certification idaho` (pos 26), `emt boise idaho` (pos 10), `emt idaho` (pos 24), `idaho emt certification` (pos 72).
Resolution: `emt-course-boise` and `emt-training-idaho` own all Idaho/Boise commercial training queries. The `/about-us/` page must be restructured to strip all commercial keyword signals from its title tag (currently "Idaho Medical Academy EMT Training - Boise ID"), H1, and body copy. The page should be re-titled for brand/entity queries only ("About Summit Medical Academy | Multi-State EMT Training"). Add internal links from the newly restructured `/about-us/` to `emt-course-boise` and `emt-training-idaho` with anchor text "EMT courses in Boise" and "EMT training in Idaho." Monitor positions on the commercial terms — expect a temporary dip as the new dedicated pages index and begin absorbing rankings.

**3. `/emt-course-in-person/` dual-intent conflict**
Competing pages: `/emt-course-in-person/` vs. `emt-continuing-education-online` (Silo 3) and `emt-course-boise` (Silo 5).
Keywords in conflict: The crawl title declares "EMT Course, Summit Medical Academy Boise ID" but the H1 reads "24 Hour EMT CE Course" — the page is simultaneously targeting initial EMT certification seekers and licensed EMTs seeking CE credits.
Resolution: Restructure `/emt-course-in-person/` (now `emt-course-in-person` in Silo 5) as exclusively an initial certification / in-person skills session page for the Boise market. Remove all CE/recertification framing from H1, body, and meta description. The CE audience is served by `emt-continuing-education-online` (Silo 3). Add an internal link to `emt-continuing-education-online` with anchor text "EMT continuing education and recertification" for visitors who arrive on this page needing CE rather than initial certification.

**4. `emt-course-cost` (committed, deprecated) duplicates `online-emt-course/cost`**
Competing pages: committed `emt-course-cost` slug vs. committed `online-emt-course/cost` slug.
Keywords in conflict: `emt training cost` (2,400/mo, $5.15 CPC), `emt course cost` (2,400/mo, $5.15 CPC).
Resolution: `online-emt-course/cost` is the canonical cost page and is being built (in_progress status in committed architecture). `emt-course-cost` is listed as deprecated in the committed architecture and should be redirected to `online-emt-course/cost` once the cost page is live. Do not build both. See Deprecation Candidates.

**5. `why-you-should-get-emt-certified` vs. `why-become-an-emt`**
Competing pages: existing `/why-you-should-get-emt-certified/` vs. committed `why-become-an-emt`.
Keywords in conflict: `should i be an emt` (pos 9, 60/mo), `benefits of emt` (pos 16, 80/mo), `why become an emt` (pos 63, 60/mo).
Resolution: The existing `/why-you-should-get-emt-certified/` page is the committed `why-become-an-emt` page — they target the same intent. Optimize the existing URL rather than creating a duplicate. If the slug is changed to `why-become-an-emt`, apply a 301 from the old URL. Do not build a separate new page.

**6. `how-to-become-an-emt-in-idaho` (committed) duplicates `become-an-emt` (Silo 4)**
Competing pages: committed `how-to-become-an-emt-in-idaho` vs. `become-an-emt` (Silo 4).
Keywords in conflict: `how to become an emt in idaho` (pos 31, 10/mo).
Resolution: `become-an-emt` is a national pathway page with Idaho as one state section (H2-level). A standalone Idaho-only pathway page is not warranted by the 10/mo keyword volume. `how-to-become-an-emt-in-idaho` should be deprecated and redirected to `become-an-emt` once that page is live. See Deprecation Candidates.

**7. Out-of-scope pages creating topical dilution**
Pages: `/pharmacy-technician-program/`, `/a-day-in-the-life-of-a-pharmacy-technician/`, `/the-process-of-becoming-a-phlebotomist/`, `/common-injuries-found-in-working-and-hunting-dogs/`, `/common-mountain-biking-injuries/`.
These pages rank for queries from competing verticals (pharmacy technician, phlebotomy, pet injuries) and dilute SMA's topical authority as an EMT training provider. `/pharmacy-technician-program/` ranks for `ultimate medical academy pharmacy technician` (pos 71, HIGH competition) — an active brand confusion signal routing a competitor's brand searches to SMA's domain. Resolution: Apply `noindex` to all five pages immediately. If any carry inbound links with meaningful equity, apply 301 redirects to the homepage rather than leaving them as noindexed pages. Do not redirect them to in-scope pages, as the traffic and link signals are from irrelevant verticals.

---

## Internal Linking Strategy

**Pillar-to-cluster patterns by silo:**

**Silo 1 (Online EMT Course):** `online-emt-course` pillar links to all seven state cluster pages (`online-emt-course/california`, `/washington`, `/oregon`, `/utah`, `/arizona`, `/montana`, `/idaho`) with anchor text pattern "Online EMT course in [State]." Each state cluster links back to the pillar with anchor text "national online EMT course." `online-emt-course/cost` and `how-online-emt-training-works` receive links from the pillar with anchors "EMT course cost and payment options" and "how online EMT training works." `emt-b-online-course` links to the pillar with anchor "complete online EMT-B course."

**Silo 2 (AEMT Course):** `aemt-course-online` pillar links to all six expansion state clusters plus `what-is-aemt`, `aemt-vs-emt`, `aemt-certification-requirements`, and `upcoming-advanced-emt-classes`. Each state cluster links back to the pillar. `aemt-vs-emt` links to both `aemt-course-online` (AEMT path) and `online-emt-course` (EMT-B path) — this is the primary cross-silo link and should use anchor text "online EMT course" for the Silo 1 link.

**Silo 3 (EMT Continuing Education):** `emt-continuing-education-online` pillar links to all six state recertification cluster pages with anchor text "EMT recertification in [State]." `emt-recertification-requirements` receives a link from the pillar with anchor "NREMT recertification requirements." Each state recertification page should cross-link to the corresponding state training page in Silo 5 (e.g., `emt-recertification/california` → `emt-training-california`) with anchor "EMT training in California" to connect the initial-certification and renewal funnels.

**Silo 4 (NREMT Test Prep):** `nremt-test-prep` pillar links to `nremt-exam-guide`, `nremt-exam-locations`, `emt-career-outcomes`, and `become-an-emt`. `why-become-an-emt` links to `nremt-test-prep` with anchor "NREMT test prep course." `become-an-emt` links to `online-emt-course` (Silo 1) as the primary enrollment CTA — this is the most important cross-silo link for converting informational pathway-research traffic into course leads.

**Silo 5 (State and Regional EMT Training):** `emt-training-idaho` pillar links to all six expansion state cluster pages and to `emt-course-boise` and `emt-course-in-person`. Each expansion state cluster page links to the corresponding `online-emt-course/[state]` page (Silo 1) with anchor "online EMT course in [State]" — this is the primary cross-silo bridge for in-state search demand finding SMA's online delivery model. Each state page should also link to the corresponding `emt-recertification/[state]` page (Silo 3) with anchor "EMT recertification in [State]."

**Silo 6 (Enrollment Support and Trust):** `about-us` links to `emt-course-boise`, `online-emt-course`, and `aemt-course-online` as the three core service entry points. `q-a` should link to `online-emt-course/cost`, `payment-plan-options`, and `online-emt-course` for every FAQ question with an enrollment conversion angle. `payment-plan-options` should link to `online-emt-course/cost` (canonical cost page) and to both `online-emt-course` and `aemt-course-online` for enrollment CTAs.

**Cross-silo links that reinforce topical authority:**
- `aemt-vs-emt` (Silo 2) → `online-emt-course` (Silo 1): reinforces EMT-B vs AEMT differentiation; anchor "online EMT course"
- `become-an-emt` (Silo 4) → `online-emt-course` (Silo 1): converts pathway-research traffic; anchor "enroll in our online EMT course"
- Each state cluster in Silo 5 → corresponding state cluster in Silo 1: connects training queries to online delivery queries
- Each state cluster in Silo 3 → corresponding state cluster in Silo 5: connects renewal audience to initial-certification content for upsell/cross-sell
- `nremt-test-prep` (Silo 4) → `aemt-course-online` (Silo 2): captures students who have passed NREMT and are considering AEMT advancement; anchor "advance to AEMT"

**Orphan risk pages (currently zero inlinks in crawl — all pages):**
All fifteen crawled pages report zero unique inlinks due to JavaScript navigation rendering. After the navigation crawlability fix is implemented, the following pages are highest orphan risk because they are not likely to appear in primary navigation:
- `nremt-exam-locations` — must receive links from `nremt-test-prep` and `q-a`
- `emt-career-outcomes` — must receive links from `why-become-an-emt`, `q-a`, and the online EMT course pillar
- `upcoming-advanced-emt-classes` — must receive links from `aemt-course-online` and the site's course schedule section
- All new state cluster pages in Silos 1, 2, 3, and 5 — must receive links from their respective pillar pages on publication; do not publish state pages before the pillar pages that link to them exist

---

executive_summary:
Summit Medical Academy's organic footprint is anchored almost entirely in Boise/Idaho market terms, a geography that belongs to the sister brand Idaho Medical Academy, not to SMA's expansion mandate. The site ranks for 70 keywords at a mean position of 46, with the strongest signals concentrated on `/aemt-course-online/` (22 keywords, 1,940 monthly volume) and `/about-us/` (12 keywords, 830 monthly volume). The about page is absorbing high-intent commercial queries — `emt training boise` (pos 8), `boise emt training` (pos 9), `idaho emt courses` (pos 74), `emt training idaho` (pos 16) — that it cannot convert. The single largest recoverable gap is the absence of a dedicated online EMT-B course page: `online emt course` (9,900/mo, $6.31 CPC) and `emt training online` (9,900/mo, $6.31 CPC) are both unranked, while the closest landing candidate, `/aemt-course-online/`, ranks at position 74 for `emt course online` (2,400/mo) — a structural mismatch between URL and user intent. SMA's six designated expansion states — Washington, Oregon, California, Utah, Arizona, and Montana — contribute zero tracked rankings and have zero corresponding pages.

The architecture organizes around four content silos: Online EMT Course (national hub + seven state cluster pages), AEMT Course (national hub + state clusters), EMT Continuing Education and Recertification (national pillar + state recertification clusters), and NREMT Test Prep and Career Support (single-pillar with supporting content). A fifth silo consolidates State and Regional EMT Training under one geo-agnostic pillar, absorbing the commercial intent currently misrouted through `/about-us/` and scattered across event URLs. A sixth silo addresses Enrollment Support and Trust, housing FAQ, cost, and comparison content that supports decision-stage queries across all service categories. The highest single-page priority is the `/online-emt-course/` national hub, which must be built before state variants go live so internal link authority can flow down the hub-and-spoke structure. The committed architecture's 28 pages are fully absorbed across these six silos; seven previously deprecated entries have been evaluated and retained with corrected roles, and two gap items identified in the Content Gap Intelligence — AEMT geo pages for Oregon and Montana, and the national CE pillar — are addressed by existing committed pages that the gap analysis did not recognize.

The site runs on WordPress with a confirmed structural crawlability problem: all fifteen crawled pages report zero unique inlinks, indicating JavaScript-rendered navigation that prevents Googlebot from discovering internal link structure via static HTML. Two blocking issues must be resolved before any new page is published: (1) the XML sitemap must be generated via Yoast SEO or Rank Math and submitted to Google Search Console, or new pages will not be reliably crawled; (2) brand entity consolidation must be completed — nine of fifteen pages carry "Idaho Medical Academy" in title tags on the `summitmedicalacademy.com` domain, creating an entity resolution failure that suppresses authority for every new page built on top of it. These are not Phase 2 tasks. Additionally, the homepage hero WebP image at 4.8MB is a probable LCP failure at the template level; every state landing page will inherit this liability if the image compression issue is not resolved before the expansion build begins. Phase 2 expansion (city-level pages below state hubs, AEMT cost pages per state, Idaho recertification page) is noted in the appropriate silos and deferred until Phase 1 state infrastructure is indexed and showing ranking signal.
